name: Debug Failed Training Run
description: Triage a failed training run with scoped evidence collection, root-cause hypotheses, minimal reproduction planning, and verifier-ready findings.

inputs.description: Run id, job id, experiment name, or local output directory for the failed run.
Failure log, stderr excerpt, traceback, or artifact path to inspect.
Training configuration, launch command, or config path associated with the run.
Expected result or prior working behavior to compare against.

permissions.description: Read logs, configs, metrics, checkpoints metadata, environment snapshots, and dataset metadata for the failed run.

approval_points.description: Confirm the debugging scope, evidence sources, non-goals, and privacy-sensitive artifacts.
Confirm the bounded reproduction plan before any rerun or command execution happens.
Share root-cause findings, remediation notes, or runbook changes outside the local workspace.

phases.name: Intake
phases.objective: Capture failed run identity, symptoms, expected behavior, constraints, and debugging boundaries.

phases.name: Artifact Collection
phases.objective: Collect logs, configs, dataset references, environment details, checkpoints metadata, and prior metrics.

phases.name: Failure Triage
phases.objective: Classify the failure mode, inspect config and code assumptions, and rank root-cause hypotheses.

phases.name: Minimal Repro Plan
phases.objective: Design a smallest useful reproduction or dry-run path with expected observations and stop conditions.

phases.name: Remediation Readiness
phases.objective: Produce the likely fix path, validation checklist, residual risk, and evidence-backed report.

required_outputs.description: Failed run id, symptom, expected behavior, scope, and non-goals.
Relevant log excerpts, error class, first failure point, and artifact links.
Runtime, dependency versions, hardware class, environment variables summary, and seeds.
Dataset source, revision, split mapping, preprocessing assumptions, and fingerprint when available.
Ranked hypotheses with supporting and contradicting evidence.
Training arguments, model, data, optimizer, scheduler, precision, and logging configuration.
Minimal reproduction or dry-run plan with commands, expected observations, and stop criteria.
Minimal dry-run command, output, and exit status when available.
Likely fix, validation checklist, rollback notes, and residual risks.
Root-cause summary, evidence links, recommended fix, and next-step decision.

artifacts.description: Pointers to logs, configs, environment snapshots, dataset metadata, and metrics used in the investigation.
Hypotheses, observations, rejected explanations, and remaining uncertainty.
Minimal reproduction plan and validation expectations.

verifiers.description: Debugging scope, failed run identity, expected behavior, and non-goals are explicit.
Environment, dependency versions, hardware class, seeds, and relevant runtime settings are recorded.
Dataset source, revision, split mapping, preprocessing assumptions, and fingerprint are recorded when relevant.
Model, data, optimizer, scheduler, precision, and logging configuration are captured.
Root-cause hypotheses cite logs, configs, artifacts, commits, or prior run evidence.
Relevant training entrypoints, modules, or config loaders can be imported or inspected in isolation.
Minimal reproduction or dry-run path has expected command, output, and exit status evidence.
Residual risks, rejected hypotheses, and validation limits were reviewed.
Debug report conclusions cite logs, configs, reproduction plan, and remediation evidence.
